assertions.title: Start Encode Initiates FFmpeg Process

steps.action: Connect to Electron app via CDP port 9222

steps.observed: Page is blank - React app failed to mount

evidence.consoleErrors: TitleBar component error: 'An error occurred in the <TitleBar> component'

assertions.issues: BLOCKED: When connecting via CDP to Electron app, window.electronAPI (exposed via contextBridge in preload script) is not available. This causes TitleBar component to crash when calling window.electronAPI.isMaximized(). No error boundary exists to handle this gracefully, causing entire React app to fail mounting.

blockers.description: window.electronAPI is undefined when connecting via CDP to Electron app. The preload script exposes electronAPI via contextBridge, but CDP connections bypass this. TitleBar component calls window.electronAPI.isMaximized() which throws TypeError. No error boundary exists to handle this, causing entire React app to fail mounting.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Restarted Electron app with --remote-debugging-port=9222, reconnected agent-browser, tried navigating to app URL - same result. window.electronAPI remains undefined in CDP context.

summary: Tested 13 assertions: All BLOCKED. The Electron app cannot be tested via CDP connection because window.electronAPI (exposed via contextBridge preload script) is not available in CDP context. This causes TitleBar component to crash when it tries to call window.electronAPI.isMaximized(), and without an error boundary, the entire React app fails to mount leaving a blank page.
